verses.1: Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest,
verses.3: A whip is for the horse,
verses.4: Don’t answer a fool according to his folly,
verses.5: Answer a fool according to his folly,
verses.6: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool
verses.7: Like the legs of the lame that hang loose,
verses.8: As one who binds a stone in a sling,
verses.9: Like a thorn bush that goes into the hand of a drunkard,
verses.10: As an archer who wounds all,
verses.11: As a dog that returns to his vomit,
verses.12: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes?
verses.13: The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road!
verses.14: As the door turns on its hinges,
verses.15: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish.
verses.16: The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes
verses.17: Like one who grabs a dog’s ears
verses.18: Like a madman who shoots torches, arrows, and death,
verses.19: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”
verses.20: For lack of wood a fire goes out.
verses.21: As coals are to hot embers,
verses.22: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels,
verses.23: Like silver dross on an earthen vessel
verses.24: A malicious man disguises himself with his lips,
verses.25: When his speech is charming, don’t believe him,
verses.26: His malice may be concealed by deception,
verses.27: Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it.
verses.28: A lying tongue hates those it hurts;
